creator: diizzy@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Olivier Duchateau from comment #9)
Let's have a look,

https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/buglist.cgi?email1=duchateau.olivier%40gmail.com&email2=diizzy%40freebsd.org&emailassigned_to2=1&emaillongdesc2=1&emailreporter1=1&emailtype1=substring&emailtype2=substring&list_id=781368&query_format=advanced

246334 - No response on feedback and superseded by direct commit to tree (not by me)
249942 - Superseeded by direct commit to tree (not by me)
249941 - Helped you out on this one
249879 - Stalled, superseded with several new patches including myself and other committers
260024 - Patch committed by other committer
249391 - Superseded by commit direct to tree (not by me)
249882 - No response on feedback and superseded by direct commit to tree (not by me)
249883 - Superseded by commit direct to tree (not by me)
251739 - Superseded by commit direct to tree (not by me)
251692 - Used your port as a base and improved it, abandoned by commit direct to tree (newer version) (not by me)
266585 - Committed by another committer
271611 - Committed by another committer
271608 - Opened 271689 by mistake, modified patch committed
272045 - Superseeded by direct commit to tree (not by me)
273906 - Closed by yourself
267344 - Never committed, port removed
282969 - Duplicate of 282932
274492 - Superseded by your own PR 283632 (I submitted 283554 by mistake)
274393 - Superseded by commit direct to tree (not by me)
275201 - No response to feedback, superseded by 279741 (newer version)

Multiple PRs of the same port (and version) isn't of course intentional and sometimes Bugzilla doesn't show up similar results when creating one or you may have simply have prepped a bunch of PRs days ahead and waiting on Poudriere runs to finish.

What's common is that you provide very little to no information about changes and in most cases nothing about any actual testing neither build or runtime. It has been requested before and likely the cause why many of your PRs don't get committed in the end or just sitting there. Also, if you've submitted an update for a port reuse the same open PR rather than creating duplicates. As Gleb mentioned most of not all work is free so streamlining efforts helps a lot.

Best regards,
Daniel
